description: Add a dedicated Activity Scheduling system with intelligent mobility-based filtering. Residents have a mobility_status field (independent, walking_aid, wheelchair, bed_bound) that determines which activities they can participate in. Activities have a required_mobility_level. The system prevents scheduling bed-bound or wheelchair residents for high-mobility activities. Includes a full-featured Activities page with a weekly calendar, activity creation, smart resident matching, and wellbeing tracking.
steps: Step 1 - Database schema: Add a new migration file backend/migrations/002_activities.sql that creates: (a) An 'activities' table (id UUID, care_home_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, description TEXT, activity_type VARCHAR, required_mobility_level VARCHAR ENUM-like via CHECK: 'any','walking_aid_or_better','wheelchair_or_better','independent_only', duration_minutes INT, max_participants INT, location VARCHAR, facilitator VARCHAR, recurring BOOLEAN, recurrence_pattern VARCHAR, category VARCHAR, sensory_friendly BOOLEAN, cognitive_level VARCHAR, notes TEXT, created_at, updated_at). (b) An 'activity_sessions' table (id UUID, care_home_id UUID FK, activity_id UUID FK, session_date DATE, start_time TIME, end_time TIME, status VARCHAR CHECK 'scheduled','in_progress','completed','cancelled', facilitator_id UUID FK users, notes TEXT, mood_rating_avg DECIMAL, created_at, updated_at). (c) An 'activity_participants' table (id UUID, session_id UUID FK, resident_id UUID FK, attendance VARCHAR CHECK 'registered','attended','declined','unable', engagement_level VARCHAR CHECK 'high','medium','low','none', mood_before VARCHAR, mood_after VARCHAR, notes TEXT, created_at). (d) Add 'mobility_status' column to residents table: ALTER TABLE residents ADD COLUMN mobility_status VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'independent' CHECK (mobility_status IN ('independent','walking_aid','wheelchair','bed_bound')). (e) Add 'interests' TEXT[] column to residents table for matching. (f) Add 'wellbeing_score' INTEGER column (0-10) to residents table.

Step 2 - Backend controller: Create backend/src/controllers/activities.controller.ts with: listActivities (GET /activities - list all activities for care home), getActivity (GET /activities/:id), createActivity (POST /activities), updateActivity (PATCH /activities/:id), deleteActivity (DELETE /activities/:id), listSessions (GET /activities/sessions - filter by date range), createSession (POST /activities/sessions - validate participant mobility), getSessionParticipants (GET /activities/sessions/:id/participants), addParticipant (POST /activities/sessions/:id/participants - CHECK mobility_status against required_mobility_level before adding, return 400 with explanation if incompatible), updateParticipant (PATCH /activities/sessions/:sessionId/participants/:residentId - update attendance/engagement), removeParticipant (DELETE /activities/sessions/:sessionId/participants/:residentId), getEligibleResidents (GET /activities/:id/eligible-residents - returns all residents whose mobility_status meets the activity's required_mobility_level), getResidentActivityHistory (GET /residents/:id/activities - activity history for a resident), getWellbeingDashboard (GET /activities/wellbeing-dashboard - aggregate stats on participation, mood improvements, engagement levels). The mobility check logic: 'any' allows all; 'walking_aid_or_better' allows independent + walking_aid; 'wheelchair_or_better' allows independent + walking_aid + wheelchair; 'independent_only' allows only independent.

Step 3 - Backend routes: Add all activity routes to backend/src/routes/index.ts following the existing pattern. Activities CRUD is isStaff, creating/editing sessions is isStaff (activities coordinator role should have access).

Step 4 - Seed data: Update backend/src/utils/seed.ts to: (a) Set mobility_status for each resident based on their existing mob field in residentsData (map 'Bed-bound' -> 'bed_bound', 'Hoist required' -> 'bed_bound', 'Wheelchair' -> 'wheelchair', 'Zimmer frame'/'Walking frame'/'Walking stick' -> 'walking_aid', 'Independent'/'Independent with...' -> 'independent'). (b) Add interests arrays to residents. (c) Seed 12-15 diverse activities covering physical, creative, social, cognitive, sensory categories with varying mobility requirements. (d) Seed 30 days of activity sessions with participant records showing engagement and mood data. (e) Set wellbeing_scores for residents.

Step 5 - Frontend types: Add Activity, ActivitySession, ActivityParticipant, and WellbeingStats types to frontend/src/types/index.ts. Add mobility_status and interests and wellbeing_score to the Resident interface.

Step 6 - Frontend hooks and API: Add activities API methods to frontend/src/services/api.ts (activitiesApi object with all CRUD + session + participant methods). Add corresponding React Query hooks to frontend/src/hooks/index.ts (useActivities, useActivity, useCreateActivity, useUpdateActivity, useSessions, useCreateSession, useSessionParticipants, useAddParticipant, useUpdateParticipant, useEligibleResidents, useResidentActivityHistory, useWellbeingDashboard).

Step 7 - Activities page: Create frontend/src/pages/Activities.tsx - a full-featured page with: (a) A weekly calendar view showing scheduled sessions with color-coded categories. (b) Activity library panel showing all available activities with mobility requirement badges. (c) Session creation modal that auto-filters eligible residents based on mobility. (d) Smart suggestions panel showing which residents haven't participated recently and would benefit from engagement. (e) Participant management with attendance tracking and mood/engagement recording. (f) A 'Wellbeing Impact' summary card showing average mood improvement, participation rates, and highlights. Use inline styles matching existing codebase patterns.

Step 8 - Activities Dashboard Widget: Create frontend/src/components/ActivitiesWidget.tsx - a dashboard widget for the Manager and Activities role dashboards showing today's scheduled activities, participation stats, and residents who haven't had activities in 3+ days. Add this widget to the ManagerDashboard.

Step 9 - Resident Detail integration: Update frontend/src/pages/ResidentDetail.tsx to add a new 'Activities' tab showing the resident's activity history, engagement patterns, mood trends from activities, and recommended activities based on their mobility status and interests.

Step 10 - App routing and navigation: Add the /activities route to frontend/src/App.tsx Routes, import the Activities page, and add 'Activities' to the NAV_ALL array (under the Care section, icon: '🎯', roles: all staff).
acceptance_criteria: Backend compiles without errors (npm run build in backend/)
Frontend compiles without errors (npm run build in frontend/)
Activity scheduling enforces mobility-based restrictions (bed_bound residents cannot be added to activities requiring walking_aid_or_better)
Activities page shows a weekly calendar with session management
Eligible residents endpoint filters by mobility_status correctly
Resident detail page shows activity history tab
Activities navigation item appears in sidebar for all staff roles
Seed data includes activities, sessions, and participant records with mobility statuses set
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/backend && npm run build' - must succeed
Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/frontend && npm run build' - must succeed
findings: Backend has 14 pre-existing TS errors (auth.controller, photos.controller, index.ts compression, middleware/upload multer types). Frontend has 19 pre-existing TS errors (CareNotes, Emar, Incidents, Training - all in unrelated files). My changes reduced frontend errors from 36 to 19 by adding missing fields to Resident type. Both builds succeed (exit code 0 with only type errors that were already present). No database running so SQL migration is compile-validated only.
